title: ampelocissus angolensis
intro: Ampelocissus angolensis, also known as the Angolan grape-vine or wild grape, is a species of flowering plant in the grape family. It is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Africa, for its various medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Ampelocissus angolensis include treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as being used as a natural remedy for wounds and skin irritations. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into a decoction or infusion, which can be taken orally or applied topically to the affected area.
